Question: Should patients with RA on DMARDs who are in low disease activity gradually taper off DMARDs, abruptly withdraw DMARDs, or continue DMARDS at the same doses?
P: adults with rheumatoid arthritis (RA) who are on stable conventional synthetic or biologic DMARD therapy and have achieved sustained low disease activity (e.g., DAS28-CRP ≤3.2 for ≥6 months)
C: continue DMARDs at the same doses

disease flare rate: proportion of patients experiencing disease flare within 6 months
sustained low disease activity or remiss: proportion of patients maintaining low disease activity or remission at 12 months
adverse events: incidence of serious infections, malignancy, or cardiovascular events
